projects.title: Mapeamento de Acessibilidade - Jardim das Americas
BRA - Untagged Crossing Ways
projects.description:
This challenge is all about finding
crossing ways that are missing a crossing tag. A crossing tag tells us
whether the crossing is marked or unmarked. For example, if the crossing
is a zebra crossing, the crossing tag would be zebra - thats my
favorite kind of crossing!

Its important to tag crossing ways with a crossing tag so that other
map users know what type of crossing it is and what markings to expect.
That way, they can stay safe and cross the road safely.

This challenge will help us identify crossing ways that are missing a
crossing tag so that we can fix them. We are going to use satellite
imagery to see if we can tell whether the crossing is marked or unmarked.
If we can, we will add a crossing tag to the crossing way.

But if we can not tell from the satellite imagery whether the crossing is
marked or unmarked, we will leave the crossing way untagged. Its better
to leave a crossing way untagged than to add a wrong crossing tag.

So, if you are up for a challenge, join us in our quest to make all crossing
ways safe and accessible for everyone. Crikey, its going to be a beaut!

projects.title: BRA - Cycleway Missing Foot Tags
projects.description:
This challenge identifies all cycleways that are missing
information about access permission for pedestrians. Mappers are asked to add a foot tag to the
cycleway to clearly identify whether foot traffic is allowed on the cycleway. Please see [Examples
from the highway=cycleway wiki page](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Tag:highway%3Dcycleway#Examples)
to learn more about how to add a foot tag to the cycleway.

projects.title: BRA - Conflicting Crossing Tags
projects.description:
This challenge is all about finding
crossing ways that have conflicting crossing tags. That means that either
the crossing tag and crossing:markings tag do not agree with each other or
perhaps the footway tag is inconsistent with the highway tag.

For example, if the crossing tag is set to zebra, but the crossing:markings
tag is set to unmarked, that means that the crossing is tagged as both
marked and unmarked. Thats no good - have you ever seen a zebra with no
markings?!?!

This challenge will help us identify crossing ways that have conflicting
crossing tags so that we can fix them. We are going to use satellite imagery
to see if we can tell whether the crossing is marked or unmarked. If we can,
we will change the crossing tag and crossing:markings tag so that they match.

But if we can not tell from the satellite imagery whether the crossing is
marked or unmarked, we will leave the crossing tag and crossing:markings tag
as they are. Its better to leave a tag blank than to add a wrong tag.

Remember, its important to tag crossing ways correctly so that other map
users know what type of crossing it is and what markings to expect. That
way, they can stay safe and cross the road safely.

So, if you are up for a challenge, join us in our quest to make all crossing
ways safe and accessible for everyone. Crikey, its going to be a beaut!

projects.title: BRA - Sidewalk Should be Crossing
projects.description:
This challenge identifies potential
pedestrian crosswalks that have been incorrectly labeled as sidewalks.
We assess the length of each sidewalk. If a sidewalk is notably short and
contains a single intermediate node marked as a 'crossing', it is highly
likely that the sidewalk is a crossing.

When we discover a sidewalk that should be classified as a crosswalk, we flag
it as a task in Map Roulette.

Human review of these tasks is crucial because there are numerous locations
where the crosswalk is not clearly defined. A combination of human review and
street-side photos is necessary for accurate mapping of the crossing.

When you are directed to the editor to make modifications, you may need to
zoom in to identify the issue. We recommend using the '+' button to zoom in,
as the area of interest will be centered on the map.

projects.title: BRA - Suspicious Unmarked Crossing Ways
projects.description:
The goal of this challenge is to identify and improve the mapping of unmarked crossings on busy or high-traffic roads.
Unmarked crossings can be dangerous for pedestrians and other non-motorized road users, as they may not be easily visible to drivers.
By identifying and improving the mapping of these crossings, we can help make our streets safer and more accessible for everyone.
Task Description:
For this challenge, we will be focusing on ways that are tagged as crossing=unmarked and cross busy or potentially high traffic roads.
Your task is to verify that these crossings exist and are accurately mapped in OpenStreetMap. If the crossing does not exist or is not accurately mapped, please make the necessary changes to the map.
To complete this challenge, verify that an actual crossing exists as represented by the OSM feature and that it is accurately tagged.
If the crossing does not exist or is not accurately mapped, make the necessary changes to the map.
- If the crossing exists (there are curb cuts or other indications) and there are no markings, you may leave the crossing as is.
- If the crossing exists and is indicated by different surface types, change the crossing tag to crossing=marked and add a crossing:markings=surface tag.
- If the crossing exists and is marked, change the crossing tag to marked, uncontrolled, or traffic_signals as appropriate.
- If no formal crossing exists but it looks like a place where people cross the road, change the crossing tag to crossing=informal and change the highway tag to something other than crossing. This helps distinguish between unmarked official crossings and informal crossings.
- If a crossing does not exist and would be dangerous for pedestrians to cross at this location, delete the crossing.

By completing tasks in this challenge, you will be helping to make OpenStreetMap more accurate and useful for pedestrians and other non-motorized road users. Good luck!

projects.title: BRA - Residential Road Foot No tags
projects.description:
This challenge focuses on reviewing and correcting the usage of foot=no tags on residential roads in OSM.
Mappers should aim to verify if the foot=no tag is correctly used, reserving it for cases where access on
foot or by pedestrians is prohibited (private roads) or obviously dangerous for foot traffic (like when a residential road meets a divided highway)
If not valid, update the sidewalk:both tag instead or use sidewalk:left/right when appropriate.
Aerial and street-level imagery are recommended to assist in verifying these tags accurately.
Your contributions will enhance the quality of OSM data and support various applications relying on this information.

projects.title: Brazil - Fix Invalid Intersections
projects.description:
This challenge is designed to help mappers
resolve highway intersection issues. The tasks included in this challenge
feature intersecting highways at equivalent layers, but without a shared node
at the intersection. When two highways intersect at the same layer, it usually
indicates that they are routable, meaning cars or pedestrians can travel from
one way to the other. However, if the two ways don't share a node at the
intersection, it can be difficult for routing software or human mappers to
determine whether the intersection is truly routable. The mapper's task is to
determine whether the two highways are on the same layer, and tag them
accordingly. In some cases, two intersecting highways may cross over each
other at different layers. For these situations, it's important to tag the
highways with the appropriate layer, taking into account that tunnels and
bridges have implied layers of -1 and 1 respectively. If the two ways are on
the same layer and should be routable then adding an intersection node to
both ways is appropriate.

projects.title: BRA - Unattached Crossing Nodes
projects.description:
This challenge identifies crossing nodes
not connected to a highway.

Nodes with ,  and  should always
be at the intersection of two roads.

projects.title: BRA - Suspicious Sidewalk Tags
projects.description:
This challenge identifies major high-speed, multi-lane roads with the 'sidewalk=\*' tag. We want to review the sidewalk tag for accuracy. If the tag is not accurate, please edit it accordingly.
### How to map:
- If the sidewalk is mapped separately from the road, use 'sidewalk:\*=separate'
- If the sidewalk is not mapped separately from the road, use 'sidewalk:\*'
- If the sidewalk does not exist, use 'sidewalk=no'
- For more complex examples, like 'sidewalk:left=no' + 'sidewalk:right=separate', [please review the examples under 'Separate Mapped Sidewalks.'](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:sidewalk#Separately_mapped_sidewalks)
### Roads are flagged if they meet one of these criteria:
- highway=motorway
- High max speed
- High total lane count
For example, a motorway has the tag 'sidewalk=right,' but the sidewalk is actually on the left and mapped separately from the road. Simply update the tag to 'sidewalk:left=separate.'
See the [wiki on 'sidewalk=\*' for guidance.](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:sidewalk)
